annotations.description: Annotations is an unstructured key value map stored with a resource that may be set by external tools to store and retrieve arbitrary metadata.

aws.description: AWS credentials configuration for CloudWatch access.

aws_access_key_id.description: AWS access key ID to authenticate with AWS if not using a credentials secret.

aws_secret_access_key.description: AWS secret access key to authenticate with AWS if not using a credentials secret.

secret.description: Reference to a pre-created Kubernetes Secret containing AWS credentials.

includesSessionToken.description: Indicates whether the referenced secret contains a session token field.

name.description: Name of the Kubernetes Secret that stores AWS credentials.

command.description: Override the default container command used to start cloudwatch-exporter.

config.description: CloudWatch exporter configuration rendered into the container.

containerPort.description: Port exposed by the cloudwatch-exporter container.

image.description:
A docker image for cloudwatch-exporter in the format <repository>:<tag>.
Default: docker.io/prom/cloudwatch-exporter:v0.16.0

imagePullPolicy.description: Image pull policy for the cloudwatch-exporter container.

labels.description: Map of string keys and values that can be used to organize and categorize (scope and select) objects.

name.description: Name used for the cloudwatch-exporter deployment and related resources.

nodeSelector.description: Node selector for scheduling cloudwatch-exporter pods.

priorityClassName.description: PriorityClassName assigned to the cloudwatch-exporter pods to prevent them from evicting.

createClusterRole.description: Allow creating ClusterRole. If false, ClusterRole must be created manually.

createClusterRoleBinding.description: Allow creating ClusterRoleBinding. If false, ClusterRoleBinding must be created manually.

replicas.description: Number of cloudwatch-exporter replicas to deploy.

resources.description: Compute resource requests and limits for cloudwatch-exporter pods.

limits.description: Maximum compute resources allocated to cloudwatch-exporter pods.

cpu.description: CPU limit for cloudwatch-exporter pods, e.g. '500m' for 0.5 CPU cores.

memory.description: Memory limit for cloudwatch-exporter pods, e.g. '256Mi' for 256 MiB of memory.

requests.description: Minimum compute resources required for cloudwatch-exporter pods.

cpu.description: CPU request for cloudwatch-exporter pods, e.g. '250m' for 0.25 CPU cores.

memory.description: Memory request for cloudwatch-exporter pods, e.g. '128Mi' for 128 MiB of memory.

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the cloudwatch-exporter Service.

labels.description: Labels to add to the cloudwatch-exporter Service.

port.description: Port exposed by the cloudwatch-exporter Service.

portName.description: Name of the service port exposed by cloudwatch-exporter.

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the cloudwatch-exporter ServiceAccount.

automountServiceAccountToken.description: Whether the service account token should be automatically mounted into the pod.

install.description: Whether a ServiceAccount should be created.

name.description: Name of the ServiceAccount to use.

securityContext.description: SecurityContext holds pod-level security attributes. The parameters are required if a Pod Security Policy is enabled for Kubernetes cluster and required if a Security Context Constraints is enabled for Openshift cluster.

runAsUser.description:
The UID to run the entrypoint of the container process.
Defaults to user specified in image metadata if unspecified.

fsGroup.description: A special supplemental group that applies to all containers in a pod.

install.description: Whether to create a ServiceMonitor for cloudwatch-exporter.

interval.description: Set how frequently Prometheus should scrape.

metricRelabelings.description: MetricRelabelConfigs to apply to samples before ingestion.

items.description: A Prometheus MetricRelabelConfig. See https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#metric_relabel_configs for details.

relabelings.description: RelabelConfigs to apply to the scrape request before scraping.

items.description: A Prometheus RelabelConfig. See https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#relabel_config for details.

telemetryPath.description: Telemetry path used by the ServiceMonitor to scrape metrics.

tolerations.description: Tolerations allow the pod to schedule onto nodes with matching taints.
